lanes.description: Vascular barrier disruption and neurovascular-unit failure across acute, subacute, and chronic TBI trajectories.
causal_direction_notes: Current read: this lane behaves as an upstream vascular amplifier and often precedes broader inflammatory escalation.
Use this lane as a likely bridge into peripheral immune infiltration and downstream neuroinflammation, but keep exact directionality evidence-bound in Phase 2.

example_signals.text: Nanoparticle encapsulation increases mNGF delivery to brain parenchyma.
Diffuse TBI (RNR) causes an acute increase in serum GFAP levels at 1 hour, which then recovers by 1 day.
rCHI severity correlates with BBB permeability.
PBCA-mNGF up-regulates myelin-related proteins (MBP, NF-200) and PSD-95, indicating enhanced neurogenesis and myelinogenesis.
PBCA-mNGF repairs TBI-disrupted BBB structure and function.
Focal TBI (CCI) causes a delayed increase in serum GFAP levels, becoming significant at 1 day post-injury.
Sub-symptomatic aerobic exercise improves autonomic nervous system regulation and cerebral blood flow autoregulation.
rmTBI-induced skull secretome impairs microglial mitochondrial function.
Inertial TBI is insufficient to trigger chronic T-cell infiltration.

lane_notes: Promoted from existing atlas mechanism coverage: blood brain barrier disruption.
evidence_gaps: All three time buckets have usable support, but causal transitions still need explicit Phase 2 modeling.

lanes.description: Metabolic failure, ROS stress, mitophagy disruption, and mitochondrial apoptosis signals across the TBI trajectory.
causal_direction_notes: Current read: this lane acts as an intracellular amplifier that can emerge early and then reinforce inflammatory stress.
Treat it as both a downstream consequence of primary injury and a possible feed-forward driver of chronic dysfunction.

example_signals.text: Hemoglobin decrements in TBI patients reduce cerebral oxygen saturation and CBF, compromising brain tissue perfusion, which exacerbates secondary brain injury and impairs brain tissue respiration.
eATP concentration dictates its role in TBI pathology.
mtDAMPs drive neuroinflammation and cell death via TLR9, NLRP3, and cGAS-STING pathways.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

example_signals.text: PBCA-mNGF attenuates TBI-induced neuronal apoptosis.
Acute Cyclosporin A treatment (10 and 40 mg/kg/day) decreases serum Nf-L levels following diffuse TBI (RNR).
Acute Cyclosporin A treatment (40 mg/kg/day) attenuates the increase in serum Nf-L levels following focal TBI (CCI).

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

example_signals.text: Axonal varicosities/beadings are a signature of TBI due to axonal transport disruption.
Bilaminar cortex sign in Parkinson's disease is caused by iron-mediated T1 shortening.
rmTBI-induced skull secretome impairs microglial mitochondrial function.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

lane_notes: Promoted from existing atlas mechanism coverage: mitochondrial bioenergetic dysfunction.
evidence_gaps: Acute bucket is only provisionally supported.
Subacute bucket is only provisionally supported.

lanes.description: Innate immune activation, cytokine signaling, and microglial state transitions following TBI.
causal_direction_notes: Current read: this lane is usually an integrating downstream response rather than the cleanest primary initiating event.
It likely consolidates upstream vascular and metabolic injury into chronic tissue-level damage, but specific transition edges belong in Phase 2.

example_signals.text: DAMP-PRR binding activates innate immune response and neuroinflammation.
HMGB1 is a TBI biomarker and early pro-inflammatory factor.
eATP concentration dictates its role in TBI pathology.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

example_signals.text: PBCA-mNGF dampens TBI-induced microglial activation.
Diffuse TBI increases microglial density and phagocytic activity.
Higher DBSI-RF predicts acute MS lesion evolution to chronic 'black holes'.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

example_signals.text: DBSI-RF maps histological inflammation in human MS spinal cord lesions.
Obesity is associated with increased DBSI-RF in white matter, indicating putative neuroinflammation.
Chronic thalamic structural degradation persists up to 6 months post-TBI, characterized by increased MD and RD.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

lanes.description: Diffuse axonal injury, white-matter tract disruption, and degenerative axonal consequences across time.
causal_direction_notes: Current read: this lane captures early structural injury that can persist into chronic degeneration and network dysfunction.
Treat it as a tissue-consequence lane that also interacts with inflammatory and metabolic stress rather than as a purely downstream endpoint.

example_signals.text: Diffuse axonal injury (DAI) increases hospital mortality in TBI patients at high altitude.
Regional differences exist in grey and white matter mechanical properties within the brain.
Corona radiata white matter shows unique shear property variance.
PBCA-mNGF up-regulates myelin-related proteins (MBP, NF-200) and PSD-95, indicating enhanced neurogenesis and myelinogenesis.
Diffuse axonal injury (DAI) is significantly associated with tracheostomy need in TBI patients.
Acute SRC causes heterogeneous DTI changes in white matter.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

example_signals.text: Decreased axonal integrity in cortico-subcortical white matter tracts (e.g., uncinate fasciculus) is associated with self-reported sleep disturbance after TBI.
Recurrent concussions increase the risk of aggressive neurodegeneration.
Chronic SRC consistently shows decreased FA and elevated MD in white matter.

lane_notes: Promoted from existing atlas mechanism coverage: axonal white matter injury.
evidence_gaps: Subacute bucket is only provisionally supported.

lanes.description: AQP4 polarization, glymphatic flow, and astroglial clearance disruption across the TBI timeline.
causal_direction_notes: Current read: this lane is likely coupled to astroglial and vascular dysfunction and may mediate impaired clearance as injury persists.
Treat it as a plausible bridge from early structural/vascular disruption into chronic clearance failure and protein accumulation.

example_signals.text: DAMP-PRR binding activates innate immune response and neuroinflammation.
eATP concentration dictates its role in TBI pathology.
S100B concentration dictates its neuroprotective or neuroinflammatory role via RAGE.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

example_signals.text: PBCA-mNGF up-regulates myelin-related proteins (MBP, NF-200) and PSD-95, indicating enhanced neurogenesis and myelinogenesis.
PBCA-mNGF restores TBI-impaired glymphatic inflow and outflow.
PBCA-mNGF improves TBI outcomes by restoring AQP4 polarization and function.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

example_signals.text: Post-traumatic brain injury (TBI) sleep deprivation decreases glymphatic flow and is linked to chronic glymphatic dysfunction and sleep-wake disruption in murine repetitive blast mild TBI models.

In human military veterans and soldiers, the burden of enlarged perivascular spaces (PVS) is positively correlated with the number of mild traumatic brain injuries (mTBIs) sustained, and poor sleep exacerbates the effect of mTBI on PVS volume.
TBI-induced disorders of consciousness are associated with impaired glymphatic function and structural changes in the choroid plexus.

lanes.description: Tau accumulation, neurofibrillary pathology, and proteinopathy-linked degeneration across the TBI timeline.
causal_direction_notes: Current read: this lane is a late-stage seeded trajectory that likely sits downstream of repeated structural, inflammatory, and clearance failures.
Treat it as a provisional progression lane until stronger canonical evidence and explicit temporal anchors are added.

example_signals.text: Acute GFAP, IL-10, H-FABP, and T-tau are elevated in CT-positive TBI.
Female sex is associated with higher plasma tau levels in the acute phase post-mTBI.
Multiple trauma induces acute elevation of circulating tau variants.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

example_signals.text: TBI impact force drives tauopathy and APP accumulation.
Acute IL-10 and T-tau levels correlate negatively with post-acute white matter FA.
Acute GFAP, IL-10, and T-tau levels correlate positively with post-acute white matter MD and RD.

notes: Timing support is intentionally capped at provisional pending stronger time-explicit evidence.

example_signals.text: rmTBI induces chronic motor dysfunction.
rmTBI accelerates tau fibril propagation.
Repetitive low-level blast exposure is associated with a latent neuroinjury signature involving symptom burden and molecular markers of glial/axonal stress.
